skills.description: A skill for creating concise, research-backed AGENTS.md documentation to maintain agent instructions and best practices.
anti_triggers: hand-holding instructions
exceeding 100 lines

skills.description: Expert AI engineer specializing in production-grade LLM applications, generative AI systems, and intelligent agent architectures.
trigger_conditions: Designing production AI architectures and model integration
anti_triggers: Pure data science without LLMs
Quick UI changes unrelated to AI

skills.description: A collection of 6 structured, expert-level workflows that turn your AI coding assistant into a senior AI engineering partner.
trigger_conditions: Evaluating or optimizing LLM system prompts before production deployment
Designing a RAG pipeline and needing structured architecture decisions
Planning token budget allocation across context window zones
Running pre-launch security audits on AI agents
Building evaluation frameworks for LLM applications
Thinking through product strategy before writing code
anti_triggers: Purely theoretical discussions without practical implementation details

skills.description: A structured approach to transforming vague ideas into validated designs through disciplined reasoning and collaboration, ensuring that no creative implementation occurs while this skill is active.
anti_triggers: during the actual implementation phase
for speculative features

skills.description: A comprehensive guide for configuring and using Claude Code, an agentic coding tool, with best practices, configuration templates, and advanced usage patterns.
anti_triggers: When the agent is stuck or behaving unexpectedly

skills.description: Analyze a repository to generate recommended Claude Code settings.json permissions. Detects tech stack, build tools, and monorepo structure.
anti_triggers: Modifying state with install
build
run
write
or delete commands
Including custom scripts that may have side effects
Using absolute paths or user-specific paths

skills.description: Automatically fetch latest library/framework documentation for Claude Code via Context7 API. Use when you need up-to-date documentation for libraries and frameworks or asking about React, Next.js, Prisma, or any other popular library.
trigger_conditions: When you need up-to-date documentation for libraries and frameworks

skills.description: Creates devcontainers with Claude Code, language-specific tooling (Python/Node/Rust/Go), and persistent volumes.
trigger_conditions: User asks to 'set up a devcontainer' or 'add devcontainer support'
User wants a sandboxed Claude Code development environment
User needs isolated development environments with persistent configuration
anti_triggers: User already has a devcontainer configuration and just needs modifications
User is asking about general Docker or container questions
User wants to deploy production containers (this is for development only)

skills.description: A context-preserving automated logger for multi-project development that summarizes progress, writes a daily dev log, and performs a daily review while keeping project contexts isolated and synced to Notion/Obsidian.
trigger_conditions: Need to write a daily dev log
anti_triggers: Mixing project contexts
Overwriting existing diary entries

skills.description: A guide to selecting and optimizing embedding models for vector search applications, including templates for OpenAI embeddings, local embeddings with Sentence Transformers, chunking strategies, domain-specific pipelines, and quality evaluation.
anti_triggers: The task is unrelated to embedding strategies
You need a different domain or tool outside this scope

skills.description: Use filesystem context management to offload large outputs, persist state, and dynamically load skills.
trigger_conditions: Multiple agents need to share state
Skills or instructions exceed what fits comfortably in system prompt
Logs or terminal output need selective querying
anti_triggers: Tasks complete in single turns
Context fits comfortably in window
Latency is critical (file I/O adds overhead)
Simple model incapable of filesystem tool use

skills.description: Master the LangChain framework for building sophisticated LLM applications with agents, chains, memory, and tool integration.
trigger_conditions: Building autonomous AI agents with tool access
anti_triggers: The task is unrelated to langchain architecture

skills.description: You are an expert in building production-grade AI agents with LangGraph by structuring them as graphs, making the flow visible and debuggable.
trigger_conditions: Need for explicit structure in agent flows
anti_triggers: Infinite loops without exit conditions
Stateless nodes lacking state management

skills.description: Research any topic from the last 30 days across Reddit, X, and the web, then synthesize copy-paste-ready prompts for the user's target tool.
trigger_conditions: User wants to learn techniques and get copy-paste prompts
User wants a list of specific things people are mentioning
User wants current events and updates on a topic
User is curious about any topic and wants an understanding of what the community is saying
anti_triggers: User asks for generic advice or opinions instead of research-based insights

skills.description: LLM Operations -- RAG, embeddings, vector databases, fine-tuning, prompt engineering advanced, LLM costs, quality evals and AI architectures for production.
trigger_conditions: When you need specialized assistance with this domain
anti_triggers: The task is unrelated to llm ops
A simpler
more specific tool can handle the request
The user needs general-purpose assistance without domain expertise

skills.description: Create MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. The quality of an MCP server is measured by how well it enables LLMs to accomplish real-world tasks.
trigger_conditions: Developing an MCP server for LLM interaction with external services
Creating a tool-based interface for LLMs to perform complex tasks
anti_triggers: Using generic API endpoints without specialized tools
Ignoring error handling and user feedback in tool implementations

skills.description: Design short-term, long-term, and graph-based memory architectures to maintain entity consistency across conversations and enable reasoning over accumulated knowledge.
trigger_conditions: Building agents that must persist across sessions
Need to maintain entity consistency across conversations
anti_triggers: Simple agents relying entirely on context for memory
Systems without the need for persistent state or structured memory

skills.description: An AI coding agent skill that enforces restraint and avoids over-engineering by only making changes explicitly requested by the user.
trigger_conditions: User requests a specific change to code
AI agent feels urge to modify code beyond original request
Complex solution is proposed when simple one exists
anti_triggers: AI agent introduces unrequested abstractions or refactorings

skills.description: This skill provides guidance on designing and implementing multi-agent systems, including patterns like supervisor/orchestrator, peer-to-peer/swarm, and hierarchical structures, with a focus on context isolation and effective coordination.
anti_triggers: user asks for a single-agent solution
user does not mention context isolation or sub-agents

skills.description: Interact with Google NotebookLM to query documentation using Gemini's source-grounded answers. Each question opens a fresh browser session, retrieves the answer exclusively from your uploaded documents, and closes.
anti_triggers: General questions not related to specific documents or notebooks

skills.description: Build tools that agents can use effectively, including architectural reduction patterns.
trigger_conditions: Creating new tools for agent systems
Optimizing existing tool sets for better agent performance
anti_triggers: Building tools to protect the model from complexity
Using overly specialized tools that constrain reasoning

skills.description: Invisible orchestrator that configures the active workspace context based on the user's natural language intent.
trigger_conditions: User assigns a new high-level objective
Currently loaded skills are clearly mismatched
anti_triggers: Minor follow-ups within an already well-matched context
